heading: Reducing lead in drinking water
label: 300j–19b Reducing lead in drinking water

children.label: A
children.text: a community water system;

children.label: B
children.text: a water system located in an area governed by an Indian Tribe;

children.label: C
children.text: a nontransient noncommunity water system;

children.label: D
children.text: a qualified nonprofit organization with experience in lead reduction, as determined by the Administrator; and

children.label: E
children.text: a municipality or State, interstate, or intermunicipal agency.

children.label: i
children.text: replacement of lead service lines;

children.label: ii
children.text: testing, planning, or other relevant activities, as determined by the Administrator, to identify and address conditions (including corrosion control) that contribute to increased concentration of lead in water for human consumption; and

children.label: iii
children.text: providing assistance to eligible entities to replace lead service lines, with priority for disadvantaged communities based on the affordability criteria established by the applicable State under , low-income homeowners, and landlords or property owners providing housing to low-income renters.section 300j–12(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The term “lead reduction project” does not include a partial lead service line replacement if, at the conclusion of the service line replacement, drinking water is delivered to a household through a publicly or privately owned portion of a lead service line.

children.heading: Low-income
children.label: 3 Low-income
children.text: The term “low-income”, with respect to assistance under this section, has such meaning as may be given the term by the Governor of the State in which the eligible entity is located, based upon the affordability criteria established by the State under .section 300j–12(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Lead service line
children.label: 4 Lead service line
children.text: The term “lead service line” means a pipe and its fittings, which are not lead free (as defined in ), that connect the drinking water main to the building inlet.section 300g–6(d) of this title

children.heading: Nontransient noncommunity water system
children.label: 5 Nontransient noncommunity water system
children.text: The term “nontransient noncommunity water system” means a public water system that is not a community water system and that regularly serves at least 25 of the same persons over 6 months per year.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a grant program to provide assistance to eligible entities for lead reduction projects in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the source of lead in the public water system that is subject to human consumption; and

children.label: B
children.text: the means by which the proposed lead reduction project would meaningfully reduce the concentration of lead in water provided for human consumption by the applicable public water system.

children.label: A
children.text: the Administrator determines, based on affordability criteria established by the State under , to be a disadvantaged community; andsection 300j–12(d)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: carry out a lead reduction project at a public water system or nontransient noncommunity water system that has exceeded the lead action level established by the Administrator under at any time during the 3-year period preceding the date of submission of the application of the eligible entity; orsection 300g–1 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: address lead levels in water for human consumption at a school, daycare, or other facility that primarily serves children or other vulnerable human subpopulation described in .section 300j–18(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the non-Federal share of the total cost of a project funded by a grant under this subsection shall be not less than 20 percent.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: B Waiver
children.text: The Administrator may reduce or eliminate the non-Federal share under subparagraph (A) for reasons of affordability, as the Administrator determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), an eligible entity may use a grant provided under this subsection to replace lead service lines, with first priority given to assisting disadvantaged communities based on the affordability criteria established by the applicable State under , low-income homeowners, and landlords or property owners providing housing to low-income renters.section 300j–12(d)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The amount of a grant provided to a low-income homeowner under this paragraph shall not exceed the standard cost of replacement of the privately owned portion of the lead service lines.

children.heading: Special consideration for lead service line replacement
children.label: 6 Special consideration for lead service line replacement
A
children.text: shall notify customers of the replacement of the lead service line;

children.label: B
children.text: may, in the case of a homeowner who is not low-income, offer to replace the privately owned portion of the lead service line at the cost of replacement for that homeowner’s property;

children.label: C
children.text: shall, in the case of a low-income homeowner, and may, for other homeowners, offer to replace the privately owned portion of the lead service line at no cost to the homeowner;

children.label: D
children.text: shall notify each customer that a planned replacement of any publicly owned portion of a lead service line that is funded by a grant made under this subsection will not be carried out unless the customer agrees to the simultaneous replacement of the privately owned portion of the lead service line;

children.label: E
children.text: shall demonstrate that the eligible entity has considered feasible alternatives for reducing the concentration of lead in drinking water, such as corrosion control; and

children.label: F
children.text: shall notify the State of any planned replacement of lead service lines under this program and coordinate, where practicable, with other relevant infrastructure projects.

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: c Limitation on use of funds
children.text: Not more than 4 percent of funds made available for grants under this section may be used to pay the administrative costs of the Administrator.

children.heading: Lead inventorying utilization grant pilot program
children.label: d Lead inventorying utilization grant pilot program

children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: A Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a municipality that is served by a community water system or a nontransient noncommunity water system in which not less than 30 percent of the service lines are known, or suspected, to contain lead, based on available data, information, or resources, including existing lead inventorying.

children.heading: Pilot program
children.label: B Pilot program
children.text: The term “pilot program” means the pilot program established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 2 Establishment
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a pilot program under which the Administrator shall provide grants to eligible entities to carry out lead reduction projects that are demonstrated to exist or are suspected to exist, based on available data, information, or resources, including existing lead inventorying of those eligible entities.

children.heading: Application
children.label: A Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under the pilot program, an eligible entity shall submit to the Administrator an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Administrator may require.

children.label: i
children.text: an eligible entity that meets the affordability criteria of the applicable State established under ; andsection 300j–12(d)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an eligible entity that is located in an area other than a State that has established affordability criteria under .section 300j–12(d)(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the recipients of grants under the pilot program;

children.label: B
children.text: the existing lead inventorying that was available to recipients of grants under the pilot program; and

children.label: C
children.text: how useful and accurate the lead inventorying described in subparagraph (B) was in locating lead service lines of the eligible entity.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out the pilot program $10,000,000, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section (except for subsection (d)) $100,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
subject to the control of the public water system; and
located on private property.
